concepts.name: Introduction to Computers
concepts.description: History and generations of computers; characteristics of computers — speed, accuracy, storage, versatility; types — micro, mini, mainframe, supercomputer; hardware vs software; applications across domains.

concepts.name: Computer Organisation
concepts.description: CPU components — ALU, control unit, registers; memory hierarchy; input/output devices; system buses — data, address, control; the fetch-decode-execute cycle.

concepts.name: Number Systems
concepts.description: Decimal, binary, octal and hexadecimal systems; base conversion; binary arithmetic; 1's and 2's complement representation; BCD, ASCII and Unicode.

concepts.name: Boolean Logic and Logic Gates
concepts.description: Boolean algebra — AND, OR, NOT operations and De Morgan's laws; logic gates including NAND, NOR, XOR; truth tables; half adder and full adder circuits.

concepts.name: Memory and Storage Systems
concepts.description: Primary memory — DRAM, SRAM, ROM variants; secondary storage — HDD, SSD, optical media; storage units from bit to terabyte; access time vs capacity trade-offs.

concepts.name: CPU Architecture and Instruction Sets
concepts.description: Von Neumann vs Harvard architecture; instruction set architecture — RISC vs CISC; addressing modes; register file organisation; clock cycles and instruction throughput.

concepts.name: Pipelining and Cache Memory
concepts.description: Instruction pipelining and hazards — structural, data, control; pipeline stalls; cache levels (L1/L2/L3); cache mapping — direct, associative, set-associative; locality of reference.

concepts.name: Software Concepts
concepts.description: System software vs application software; operating systems, language translators, utility programs; open source vs proprietary licensing; freeware, shareware and firmware.

concepts.name: Operating System Fundamentals
concepts.description: Functions of an OS — process, memory, file and I/O management; OS types — batch, time-sharing, real-time, distributed, mobile; the role of the kernel and system calls.

concepts.name: Problem Solving Methodology
concepts.description: Stages of problem solving — analysis, algorithm design, coding, testing, documentation; problem decomposition; abstraction and generalisation as design tools.

concepts.name: Algorithms
concepts.description: Definition and properties of an algorithm — finiteness, definiteness, effectiveness; representations — natural language, pseudocode, flowchart; sequence, selection and iteration constructs.

concepts.name: Flowcharts and Pseudocode
concepts.description: Flowchart symbols and conventions; constructing flowcharts for linear, conditional and iterative logic; translating between flowcharts and pseudocode.

concepts.name: Asymptotic Notation
concepts.description: Big-O, Big-Omega and Big-Theta notation; best, worst and average case analysis; growth rate comparison of common functions; analysing simple loops and nested loops.

concepts.name: Time and Space Complexity Analysis
concepts.description: Counting elementary operations; recurrence relations for recursive algorithms; the Master theorem (informal); trade-offs between time and space efficiency.

concepts.name: Divide and Conquer
concepts.description: The divide-conquer-combine paradigm; recurrence-based analysis; classic examples — binary search, merge sort, quicksort, fast exponentiation.

concepts.name: Greedy Algorithms
concepts.description: Greedy choice property and optimal substructure; activity selection; coin change (greedy case); minimum spanning tree intuition; when greedy fails to give an optimal solution.

concepts.name: Dynamic Programming
concepts.description: Overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure; memoisation vs tabulation; classic problems — Fibonacci, knapsack, longest common subsequence, edit distance.

concepts.name: Backtracking
concepts.description: State-space search with pruning; the general backtracking template; classic problems — N-Queens, Sudoku solving, subset generation, permutations.

concepts.name: NP-Completeness and Intractability
concepts.description: P vs NP; polynomial-time reductions; NP-complete and NP-hard problems; the Cook-Levin theorem (informal); approximation and heuristic strategies for intractable problems.

concepts.name: Introduction to Programming
concepts.description: High-level vs low-level languages; compilers, interpreters and assemblers; source code vs object code; an overview of procedural, object-oriented and functional paradigms.

concepts.name: Python Language Basics
concepts.description: Interactive mode vs script mode; tokens — keywords, identifiers, literals, operators; comments; print() and input(); the role of indentation in Python syntax.

concepts.name: Data Types and Variables
concepts.description: Built-in types — int, float, complex, bool, str, NoneType; mutable vs immutable objects; variable assignment, multiple assignment and dynamic typing.

concepts.name: Operators and Expressions
concepts.description: Arithmetic, relational, logical, bitwise, assignment, membership and identity operators; operator precedence and associativity; building and evaluating compound expressions.

concepts.name: Type Conversion and Casting
concepts.description: Implicit vs explicit type conversion; int(), float(), str() and related casting functions; common pitfalls when mixing numeric and string types.

concepts.name: Input, Output and Formatting
concepts.description: input() and print() with sep/end parameters; formatted output via %-formatting, format() and f-strings; reading and validating multiple input values.

concepts.name: Exception Handling
concepts.description: try-except-else-finally blocks; raising exceptions; built-in exception hierarchy; custom exceptions; defensive programming versus exception-based control flow.

concepts.name: Conditional Statements
concepts.description: if, if-else and if-elif-else constructs; nested conditionals; short-circuit evaluation; conditional (ternary) expressions; common decision-making patterns.

concepts.name: Iteration — while and for Loops
concepts.description: while loop semantics; for loop with range() and iterables; break, continue and pass; the else clause on loops; nested loops and common iteration pitfalls.

concepts.name: Nested Control Flow Patterns
concepts.description: Combining nested loops and conditionals to solve pattern-printing, matrix traversal and search problems; flag variables and early termination strategies.

concepts.name: Strings
concepts.description: String creation and indexing; positive/negative indexing and slicing; string operators; key methods — split(), join(), strip(), replace(), find(); string immutability.

concepts.name: Lists
concepts.description: List creation, indexing and slicing; mutating methods — append(), insert(), remove(), pop(), sort(); list comprehensions; lists used as stacks and queues.

concepts.name: Tuples
concepts.description: Tuple creation, packing and unpacking; indexing and slicing; immutability and its implications; choosing tuples over lists for fixed, heterogeneous records.

concepts.name: Dictionaries
concepts.description: Key-value pair storage; accessing, updating and deleting entries; methods — keys(), values(), items(), get(), update(); iterating and nesting dictionaries.

concepts.name: Sets
concepts.description: Unordered unique-element collections; set operators — union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference; mutating methods; the immutable frozenset.

concepts.name: Comprehensions, Iterators and Generators
concepts.description: List, dict and set comprehensions; the iterator protocol (__iter__, __next__); generator functions and the yield keyword; lazy evaluation versus eager evaluation.

concepts.name: Functions
concepts.description: Defining and calling functions; positional, default and keyword arguments; *args and **kwargs; the return statement; docstrings as living documentation.

concepts.name: Scope and Namespaces
concepts.description: Local, enclosing, global and built-in (LEGB) scope resolution; the global and nonlocal keywords; namespace lifetime and closures.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.description: Base case and recursive case design; the call stack during recursive calls; classic recursive problems — factorial, Fibonacci, GCD; recursion versus iteration trade-offs.

concepts.name: Lambda Expressions and Functional Tools
concepts.description: Anonymous functions with lambda; map(), filter() and reduce(); first-class functions and higher-order functions; functional versus imperative style.

concepts.name: Modules and Packages
concepts.description: Creating and importing modules; import variants; the Python Standard Library (math, random, datetime, os); pip and third-party packages; the __name__ guard.

concepts.name: Decorators
concepts.description: Functions as first-class objects; wrapping functions to add behaviour; the @decorator syntax; practical uses — logging, timing, memoisation, access control.

concepts.name: Text File Handling
concepts.description: Need for persistent storage; open()/close() and the with statement; file modes; read(), readline(), readlines(); write() and writelines(); the file position pointer.

concepts.name: CSV File Handling
concepts.description: CSV structure and delimiters; the csv module — reader, writer, DictReader, DictWriter; handling headers; practical tabular-data workflows.

concepts.name: Binary Files and Serialisation
concepts.description: Binary versus text file modes; the pickle module — dump() and load(); object serialisation and deserialisation; persisting structured records to disk.

concepts.name: Stacks
concepts.description: LIFO discipline; push, pop and peek operations; array-based and list-based implementations; applications — expression evaluation, backtracking, undo history.

concepts.name: Queues
concepts.description: FIFO discipline; enqueue and dequeue operations; circular queues; implementation with collections.deque; applications — scheduling, breadth-first traversal.

concepts.name: Deques and Priority Queues
concepts.description: Double-ended queues and their O(1) operations at both ends; priority queue semantics; heap-backed priority queues versus naive linear-scan implementations.

concepts.name: Singly Linked Lists
concepts.description: Node structure and head pointer; traversal, insertion and deletion at arbitrary positions; trade-offs versus contiguous arrays; memory representation.

concepts.name: Doubly and Circular Linked Lists
concepts.description: Bidirectional traversal with doubly linked nodes; circular list structures; use cases — undo/redo chains, round-robin scheduling, music playlists.

concepts.name: Trees
concepts.description: Tree terminology — root, leaf, height, depth; binary trees; tree traversals — preorder, inorder, postorder, level-order; recursive tree algorithms.

concepts.name: Binary Search Trees
concepts.description: BST ordering property; search, insertion and deletion; in-order traversal yielding sorted output; degeneration to a linked list in the worst case.

concepts.name: Balanced Search Trees
concepts.description: Why unbalanced BSTs degrade to O(n); AVL tree rotations and balance factor; red-black tree invariants (conceptual); B-trees for disk-backed indexes.

concepts.name: Heaps
concepts.description: Min-heap and max-heap invariants; array representation of a binary heap; heapify, insert and extract-min/max; heap sort and priority scheduling.

concepts.name: Hashing and Hash Tables
concepts.description: Hash functions and uniform distribution; collision resolution — chaining and open addressing; load factor and dynamic resizing; average O(1) lookup versus worst case.

concepts.name: Graphs and Their Representations
concepts.description: Directed vs undirected, weighted vs unweighted graphs; adjacency matrix versus adjacency list trade-offs; degree, path, cycle and connectivity terminology.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal — BFS and DFS
concepts.description: Breadth-first search using a queue; depth-first search using recursion or a stack; visited-set bookkeeping; applications — connectivity, cycle detection, topological sort.

concepts.name: Shortest Path Algorithms
concepts.description: Dijkstra's algorithm for non-negative weights; Bellman-Ford for graphs with negative edges; relaxation as the unifying idea; limitations and correctness conditions.

concepts.name: Minimum Spanning Trees
concepts.description: Spanning tree definition and properties; Kruskal's algorithm with union-find; Prim's algorithm with a priority queue; comparing the two greedy strategies.

concepts.name: Linear Search
concepts.description: Sequential scan for unsorted data; best, worst and average case analysis; when linear search is preferable to more complex alternatives.

concepts.name: Binary Search
concepts.description: Precondition of sorted input; the halving search strategy; iterative and recursive implementations; off-by-one pitfalls; O(log n) analysis.

concepts.name: Elementary Sorting — Bubble, Selection, Insertion
concepts.description: Bubble sort, selection sort and insertion sort algorithms; in-place sorting and stability; O(n^2) comparison-based analysis; when simple sorts remain practical.

concepts.name: Efficient Sorting — Merge Sort and Quick Sort
concepts.description: Merge sort's divide-and-conquer recursion and O(n log n) guarantee; quicksort partitioning and pivot choice; average versus worst-case behaviour; stability comparison.

concepts.name: Object-Oriented Programming Concepts
concepts.description: Classes and objects; the four pillars — encapsulation, abstraction, inheritance, polymorphism; comparing OOP to the procedural paradigm.

concepts.name: Classes and Objects in Python
concepts.description: Defining classes; the __init__ constructor and self parameter; instance versus class attributes; instance methods; __str__ and __repr__ for readable objects.

concepts.name: Encapsulation and Abstraction
concepts.description: Access modifiers by convention — public, _protected, __private; property decorators for controlled attribute access; abstract base classes and interfaces.

concepts.name: Inheritance
concepts.description: Single and multiple inheritance; the super() mechanism; method resolution order; overriding versus extending base-class behaviour.

concepts.name: Polymorphism and Operator Overloading
concepts.description: Method overriding as runtime polymorphism; duck typing; overloading operators via dunder methods (__add__, __eq__, __lt__); consistent comparison semantics.

concepts.name: Class Methods and Static Methods
concepts.description: @classmethod versus @staticmethod versus instance methods; alternative constructors via classmethods; when a method belongs to the class rather than an instance.

concepts.name: Introduction to Design Patterns
concepts.description: The purpose of design patterns as reusable solutions; singleton, factory and observer patterns; composition over inheritance as a guiding principle.

concepts.name: Database Concepts
concepts.description: Data versus information; advantages of a DBMS over flat-file storage; schema, instance, relation, tuple and attribute terminology; types of database systems.

concepts.name: Entity-Relationship Modeling
concepts.description: Entities, attributes and relationships; cardinality — one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many; ER diagram notation; translating an ER model into relational tables.

concepts.name: The Relational Model
concepts.description: Tables, rows and columns as the relational structure; keys — primary, candidate, foreign, super key; entity and referential integrity constraints.

concepts.name: Normalisation
concepts.description: Redundancy and update anomalies; functional dependencies; 1NF, 2NF, 3NF and BCNF; the trade-off between normalisation and query performance.

concepts.name: SQL — DDL and DML Basics
concepts.description: SQL history and standards; DDL — CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE, DROP TABLE; DML — INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE; the WHERE clause and common SQL data types.

concepts.name: SQL — Queries and Aggregate Functions
concepts.description: SELECT with ORDER BY, GROUP BY and HAVING; aggregate functions — COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX; DISTINCT, LIKE wildcards, BETWEEN, IN and IS NULL.

concepts.name: SQL — Joins and Subqueries
concepts.description: INNER, LEFT, RIGHT and FULL OUTER joins; self joins and natural joins; correlated and non-correlated subqueries; EXISTS, UNION and views.

concepts.name: Transactions and the ACID Properties
concepts.description: Atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability; commit and rollback; concurrent transaction anomalies — dirty read, lost update; basic locking strategies.

concepts.name: Indexing and Query Optimisation
concepts.description: B-tree indexes for fast lookup; the trade-off between read speed and write/storage overhead; query execution plans; when an index helps versus hurts performance.

concepts.name: Introduction to NoSQL Databases
concepts.description: Key-value, document, column-family and graph database models; the CAP theorem trade-offs; when NoSQL is preferred over a relational schema.

concepts.name: Connecting Python to MySQL
concepts.description: mysql.connector module; establishing a connection and using a cursor; execute() and executemany(); fetchone()/fetchall(); parameterised queries to prevent SQL injection.

concepts.name: Process Management
concepts.description: Process versus program; process control block; process states — new, ready, running, waiting, terminated; context switching overhead.

concepts.name: Threads and Concurrency
concepts.description: Threads versus processes; the benefits and risks of concurrency; race conditions; the need for synchronisation primitives.

concepts.name: CPU Scheduling Algorithms
concepts.description: Scheduling criteria — turnaround, waiting time, throughput; FCFS, SJF, round robin and priority scheduling; preemptive versus non-preemptive scheduling.

concepts.name: Synchronisation and Deadlocks
concepts.description: Mutexes and semaphores; the critical-section problem; the four necessary conditions for deadlock; deadlock prevention, avoidance and detection strategies.

concepts.name: Memory Management
concepts.description: Contiguous allocation; paging and segmentation; the page table and address translation; internal versus external fragmentation.

concepts.name: Virtual Memory
concepts.description: Demand paging; page faults and page replacement policies — FIFO, LRU, optimal; thrashing and the working-set model.

concepts.name: File Systems
concepts.description: File allocation methods — contiguous, linked, indexed; directory structures; metadata and inodes; journaling for crash recovery.

concepts.name: Networking Basics
concepts.description: The need for networking; LAN, MAN, WAN and PAN; network topologies — bus, star, ring, mesh; wired versus wireless transmission media; bandwidth concepts.

concepts.name: OSI and TCP/IP Reference Models
concepts.description: The seven OSI layers and their responsibilities; the four-layer TCP/IP model; encapsulation as data passes down the stack; mapping protocols to layers.

concepts.name: IP Addressing and Subnetting
concepts.description: IPv4 address classes and notation; IPv6 motivation and format; subnet masks and CIDR notation; basic subnetting calculations.

concepts.name: DNS and DHCP
concepts.description: Domain name resolution hierarchy; how a DNS query is resolved; DHCP's role in dynamic IP allocation; the lease lifecycle.

concepts.name: Application-Layer Protocols
concepts.description: HTTP and HTTPS request-response cycle; FTP for file transfer; SMTP, POP3 and IMAP for email; comparing stateless and stateful protocol behaviour.

concepts.name: TCP and UDP — Transport Layer
concepts.description: Connection-oriented TCP — handshake, reliability, flow control; connectionless UDP — speed versus reliability trade-off; choosing a transport protocol per use case.

concepts.name: Routing and Congestion Control
concepts.description: Routers, switches and routing tables; distance-vector versus link-state routing concepts; TCP congestion control — slow start, congestion avoidance.

concepts.name: Wireless and Mobile Networks
concepts.description: Wi-Fi standards and CSMA/CA; cellular network generations; mobile IP and handoff between cells; challenges unique to wireless transmission.

concepts.name: Cloud Computing
concepts.description: IaaS, PaaS and SaaS service models; public, private and hybrid cloud deployment; elasticity and on-demand scaling; trade-offs of outsourcing infrastructure.

concepts.name: Cyber Ethics and Online Safety
concepts.description: Netiquette and digital footprint; cybercrime types — hacking, identity theft, cyberbullying, phishing; safe browsing practices; copyright and software licensing basics.

concepts.name: Cryptography Fundamentals
concepts.description: Plaintext, ciphertext and the role of keys; symmetric versus asymmetric encryption; the purpose of cryptographic hash functions; digital signatures conceptually.

concepts.name: Network and Web Security
concepts.description: TLS/SSL and the HTTPS handshake; firewalls and VPNs; common web vulnerabilities — SQL injection, XSS, CSRF; the principle of least privilege.

concepts.name: Data Protection and Privacy
concepts.description: Categorising sensitive personal data; data protection principles; cookies and tracking technologies; an overview of GDPR-style regulatory expectations.

concepts.name: The Client-Server Model
concepts.description: Roles of client and server; the request-response cycle; static versus dynamic content; how a browser resolves and renders a URL.

concepts.name: HTML and CSS Basics
concepts.description: HTML document structure and common tags; semantic markup; CSS selectors and the box model; separating content from presentation.

concepts.name: REST APIs
concepts.description: Resources and URIs; HTTP verbs — GET, POST, PUT, DELETE; status codes; JSON as a data-exchange format; statelessness as a REST constraint.

concepts.name: Finite Automata
concepts.description: Deterministic and non-deterministic finite automata; state transition diagrams; the languages a finite automaton can recognise.

concepts.name: Regular Expressions and Regular Languages
concepts.description: Regular expression syntax; the equivalence of regular expressions and finite automata; pattern matching applications in text processing.

concepts.name: Context-Free Grammars
concepts.description: Grammar productions and derivations; parse trees; the role of CFGs in describing programming-language syntax; ambiguity in grammars.

concepts.name: Turing Machines
concepts.description: The Turing machine model as a formalisation of computation; the Church-Turing thesis; the halting problem and its undecidability.

concepts.name: Computational Complexity Classes
concepts.description: The classes P and NP revisited formally; PSPACE and EXPTIME; reductions between problems; open questions in complexity theory.

concepts.name: Software Development Life Cycle
concepts.description: Waterfall, iterative and agile process models; requirements analysis; design, implementation, testing and maintenance phases.

concepts.name: Version Control with Git
concepts.description: Repositories, commits and branches; merging and resolving conflicts; the typical feature-branch workflow; the role of remotes in collaboration.

concepts.name: Software Testing Fundamentals
concepts.description: Unit, integration and system testing; test cases and coverage; black-box versus white-box testing; the cost of defects found late in the lifecycle.

concepts.name: Agile Practices and Software Design Principles
concepts.description: Agile values and iterative delivery; the SOLID design principles; the value of refactoring; balancing technical debt against delivery speed.

concepts.name: NumPy for Numerical Computing
concepts.description: ndarray versus Python lists; array creation — zeros, ones, arange, linspace; broadcasting; vectorised arithmetic; indexing and slicing multi-dimensional arrays.

concepts.name: Pandas for Data Analysis
concepts.description: Series and DataFrame structures; reading and writing CSV data; data exploration with head/info/describe; filtering, grouping and merging DataFrames.

concepts.name: Data Visualisation with Matplotlib
concepts.description: Line, bar, scatter and pie charts; histograms for distribution analysis; customising titles, labels and legends; choosing an appropriate chart for the data.

concepts.name: Introduction to Machine Learning
concepts.description: Supervised versus unsupervised learning; training and test splits; overfitting and underfitting; the bias-variance trade-off conceptually.

concepts.name: Supervised Learning Models
concepts.description: Linear and logistic regression; decision trees; the classification versus regression distinction; evaluating models with accuracy, precision and recall.

concepts.name: Unsupervised Learning Models
concepts.description: k-means clustering; hierarchical clustering; dimensionality reduction intuition (PCA); evaluating clusters without ground-truth labels.

concepts.name: Introduction to Neural Networks
concepts.description: The perceptron and the artificial neuron; activation functions; forward propagation; gradient-based learning and backpropagation at a conceptual level.
